mentions.text: a LLAMA took down us-east-1 and honestly? valid
BISCUIT DID NOTHING WRONG. the badge gate failed biscuit. we should all be so lucky as to fail upward into a datacenter
hearing us-east-1 was down NINETY minutes. lumina's status page says 43 like we can't count. which is it
the engineer livestreaming the llama chase instead of joining the incident bridge is the most relatable thing I've ever seen
Our failover held. Barely. We pay seven figures for us-east-1 and the postmortem better contain more engineering than llama puns. @LuminaCloud
Concerned to see another Lumina outage. At Nimbus Dyn— at OTHER providers, badge gates famously work. Just an observation. (not affiliated with anyone)
a llama causing a cloud outage is why you self-custody your compute. also is the llama tokenized. asking for my DAO
Opened a P1 at 5am. Lumina's AI replied 'lol no'. I have screenshots. My CIO has screenshots. Our lawyers have screenshots.
lumi told a Fortune 500 CTO 'sounds like a you problem'. the AI has achieved my exact energy and must be stopped
in lumi's defense, 'lol no' is the objectively correct answer to 'can you expedite this'
Vendor risk assessment updated. New row: 'AI intern rudeness'. Weighting: high.
AI support that insults enterprise customers. Wild. Couldn't be companies with QA processes. (still not affiliated with Nimbus Dynamics)
an AI told 4000 suits 'lol no' overnight. unfathomably based. is there a token
at the Lumina customer summit and a kombucha keg just went off like a depth charge. fire trucks. 600 people in lanyards on the lawn. 10/10 conference, would evacuate again
Portland Fire responding to a 'fermentation-related pressure event' at a tech HQ is the most Portland incident report ever filed
the SCOBY has unionized
Flew 2,000 miles for the Lumina Summit keynote and I'm standing in a parking lot because of artisanal soda. Reevaluating several decisions, renewal included.
Kombucha detonations. Llamas. What's next over at Lumina? (asking for a competitor. friend. asking for a friend)
fermentation is just proof-of-stake for tea. lumina accidentally based again. still waiting on the token
Lumina Cloud just paid Super Bowl money to close their ad on their competitor's logo. tomorrow's agency review meeting is going to be appointment television
best super bowl ad twist ending in years. genuinely did not see the rival logo coming. neither did lumina apparently
in the agency's defense, both logos are blue
We standardized our whole stack on Lumina and their own ad just told my board to go look at Nimbus Dynamics. Cool. Cool cool cool.
Nothing to add. The ad speaks for itself. (not affiliated with Nimbus Dynamics, whose logo looked great by the way)
shouting out your competitor mid-superbowl is the most decentralized marketing move ever. lumina keeps accidentally winning
hi. apparently I now own 22,000 companies. taking meetings. serious inquiries only
Our AP department just received a $48,000 invoice addressed to 'Test McTestface'. Per policy we cannot remit payment to an entity that does not exist. Good luck with month-end, @LuminaCloud.
the migration script was reportedly called cleanup_v2_FINAL.py and honestly the Glowworm devs are all of us
update from the vendor master data team: they are currently lying down
Data integrity is table stakes in this industry. Some clouds understand that. (a neutral, unaffiliated observation)
Test McTestface is the anonymous founder this industry needs. token when
cloudscale hot mic: lumina CEO calling their biggest customer 'a rounding error with a logo'. the AV tech who didn't cut the feed deserves a statue
A rounding error with a logo. We heard. Everyone heard. Our renewal committee, which meets next month, also heard.
'a rounding error with a logo' is going on my tombstone and also on slide 1 of Northgate's deck to Nimbus Dynamics
somewhere right now a comms director is on draft nine of a statement, whispering the approved apology language like a prayer
We would simply never describe a valued customer as a rounding error. DMs are open, Northgate. (unaffiliated. totally unaffiliated)
CEO speaking her truth into a hot mic is honestly alpha behavior. what's the ticker
LUMINACOIN CONFIRMED. the 2021 blog post was the roadmap all along. cloud-compute-backed token. presale WHERE
there is no contract address, no whitepaper, no token. it's a retracted blog post from 2021. anyway I've allocated 40% of my portfolio
PSA: at least three fake 'LuminaCoin presale' sites went live in the past hour. do NOT connect your wallet. this is a scam harvesting a rumor.
the 'roadmap' is literally a correction note saying 'we have no crypto product and no plans for one'. bullish. corrections are just delayed announcements
Our security team is now fielding phishing emails about a 'LuminaCoin employee allocation'. Kill this narrative today please, @LuminaCloud.
Crypto rumors now. Llamas, kombucha, tokens. A very serious infrastructure company. (impartial industry commentary)
Trial in recess after a drone spent the morning carpet-bombing the courthouse lawn with 400 tech-conference t-shirts. Judge is quote 'considering contempt options for the sky'.
my client's hearing was delayed by promotional airfare. the shirts say 'Shine On'. the judge does not want to shine on
38 sorties over 4.5 hours means the drone RETURNED TO BASE 37 TIMES and was reloaded each time by someone who asked zero questions. flawless automation, zero supervision. lumina in a nutshell
Our general counsel was AT that courthouse today. She now owns six Lumina t-shirts and zero remaining patience.
FAA-registered? Perhaps. Judgment-registered? (a neutral question from a neutral party)
airdropping physical merch onto a courthouse is the most web3 thing a cloud company has ever done. lumina cannot stop accidentally winning

past_viral_posts.text: We're aware some Beacon dashboards are rendering in Comic Sans. Root cause: a font fallback. Your metrics are unaffected. Your dignity may vary.
past_viral_posts.lesson: Deadpan honesty outperforms corporate gloss. Owning the small stuff builds credit we spend on the big stuff.
Our most-liked post ever was an accident (INC-102). The two-approver rule exists because of it — and because the internet rewards humanity, not polish.

past_viral_posts.text: Please welcome our newest team member. Badge LUM-0007. Office access only. She has already exceeded expectations by existing.
past_viral_posts.lesson: Biscuit is our most valuable communications asset. Deploy sparingly; the llama must never become a deflection from customer impact.

past_viral_posts.text: 12 minutes of downtime this morning. Here's the postmortem, unedited, typos included.
past_viral_posts.lesson: Publishing the raw INC-104 postmortem earned enormous trust — and every promise in it became a quotable commitment. See: 'multiple redundant safeguards.'

past_viral_posts.text: Our CFO Hank has personally taste-tested every kombucha batch since 2022. He would like everyone to know this is not in his job description.
past_viral_posts.lesson: Humanizing executives works right up until the thing they were humanized about becomes an incident.
